name: Lensbnb — listings to reels (OSS project intro)
description:
### About this project
Lensbnb is an open-source MCP service that turns any structured brief into a 30-second 9:16 vertical reel. It orchestrates MiniMax's full multimodal stack — M2 for the script, image-01 for AI-generated cards, speech-2.8-hd for narration, music-2.6 for the bed — and stitches everything with ffmpeg.

### What's agentic
The pipeline is not a fixed sequence of API calls. The LLM picks the voice persona from a curated catalog with rationale. It validates the script JSON against a schema and regenerates with feedback if narration is the wrong length. It reviews up to 18 candidate photos clustered by perceptual hash and picks the 5 most diverse + useful for the Ken-Burns sequence. Every decision is logged.

### Why it matters
Real-estate listings, recipes, OSS READMEs, news headlines — all reduce to a brief + photos. One MCP tool, one pipeline, many domains. Drop into Claude Desktop or any MCP client, fork the code, build your own consumer.

### Stack
TypeScript + Bun, Next.js 16, Supabase Postgres + Storage, ffmpeg with libass + libfreetype, agent-browser for ingest. MIT licensed.
_note_to_user: This is a TEMPLATE. The photo_urls above point to placehold.co (stable color blocks with text overlays) so the brief is callable end-to-end. Before generating a real OSS-project reel, replace these with screenshots, logos, architecture diagrams, or code-on-screen photos from your actual project.
